title: Living in Boulder Vlog 4 - 15 - 2017: Swam 822 meters, rode 25 miles, ran 8.6 miles

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs a full triathlon training day in Boulder: swimming 822 meters with fins and paddles, cycling 25 miles (averaging 19.2 mph on a 20-column time trial), and running 8.6 miles. He also shares purchases from a bike shop and triathlon store, notes a car sale no-show, and talks about his goal of competing in a local triathlon in August.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: plans to sign up for an August triathlon once he loses weight and builds strength to around 150 lbs
not officially competitive yet — riding a heavy old bike and still under 20 mph average
a dedicated swim bag is worth it because disorganization drains energy needed for training
tight on money and needs to budget carefully as appetite and food costs will increase with heavier training
when there are no plans, treat it as an opportunity for a big training day

claims.subject: Kyle

transcript.text: Hello, Living in Boulder Vlog for April 15th, in case anybody's wondering why I have my shirt off, it's for body transformation, for my exercise.
So, today, I got up at like 8 or something, and then I swam 54 lengths in my 50 foot pool with fins and paddles, and it destroyed me.
I have no strength when I go like this on this arm, when I push down I can have no strength.
But it was hard to even hold it, hold the form.
Yeah, but I want to get good at it.
So, I had to leave sooner, earlier.
I don't know how far 54 lengths is, I'll have to make a chart.
But I could hear the music, I think I wanted to try an audio book, a louder audio book than last time to see how it works in there.
So, then I came out, and I was waiting around because somebody was going to come and look at my car at 3, but they never called or showed up.
So, at 3.30, and while I was doing that I was working on my 20 area sizes page, putting the pictures on.
And, yeah, until 3.30 came around, and I went on a ride.
But before that, this morning when I got up, I went over to the bike shop and got some gloves, because my hands were getting kind of soared.
Three left gloves, but no, right, so I had to get a new set.
And then I went down to the triathlon store, Colorado Multisport, and got a swim bag, so I don't have to locate everything and put it in, you know, my, like, normal bag.
And then I got, like, anti-fog, the goggles, and a swim cap, and a suit, a triathlon suit, the shorts and the tank top, so.
Whew, okay, I'm ready to go.
So, and then I came back and, yeah, I went on the ride.
I was going to do my 20-kilometer time trial to find out my level.
So, I think I'm level 61, 19.2 miles an hour for 20-kilometer time trial.
That's faster than the last time I went, so after four days of riding, I'm already stronger.
I don't remember when the last time, though, it was, because even though I remember in my head putting it on there, and I even remember looking at it,
It's nowhere in my website.
So, I made sure to put what I did today on my website, because I use my website as a tracker for progress.
So, I think, yeah, anything under 20 miles an hour, you're a commuter.
So, I'm not officially a bike racer.
Although, I am doing my bicycle racing with an old 30-year-old, 25-pound or whatever, 30-pound road bike.

So, oh, but I was talking to the guys at Colorado Motorsport about bikes, because I'm going to be, as soon as I lose weight and I get strong, like 150 pounds or something, I'm going to want a faster bike.
The triathlon, I think I'm going to sign up for the triathlon after I sell my car.
It's in August and not in July, so I have four months to train for it.
So, hmm, I try to do stuff that's on my car, and then I get that, and I don't know, maybe if I can figure out.
Well, one day, yeah, I'd like to get a faster bike, but we'll figure that out.
I have too many things to do on my website first.
So, but yeah, I probably won't be really wanting it for a while.
I'm still so heavy.
So, then I came back, and then I went running.
I was going to run to the top of Mount Sinus, but I got to the base of it.
And then I, you know, remembered that I just bought a t-shirt and not like a running shirt, and I didn't have an extra shirt on.
And I knew I was going to get cold, and I went to the top.
And so that's my lesson.
Make sure to bring a shirt, should I buy a shirt.
So I came back, and on the way back, I went to the local track, running track, to check it out.
And it's totally open.
It's cool.
Anybody can use it.
And it's only like a mile from here, and it was of a junior high.
And there's even a water fountain now.
I'm getting near it.
And then I rode home.
So I rode, so I swam 50 foot lengths, I don't know how far that is.
And then I rode 25 miles, and then I ran like 8.6 miles.
And now I'm totally wasted.
I didn't listen to any music, I mean any audio books.
I'm going to do that after I run out of weed.
I only have like this much more weed left, because I gave my roommate a handful the other day.
Because he gave me two wheels.
So I'm not going to, I'm going to tell him no more weed for him.
He'll have to get his own.
We want some.
But yeah, when I run out.

And it sucks your energy too.

It's too expensive.

So, okay.
So that's my VLOG for today.
Tomorrow is Sunday.

So my new general rule of thumb is when there's no plans, try to make it a big day.
So we'll see if I can, I'd like to swim in the morning.

Peace out.
